feedback.text: Because the digital fairness act is aimed at protecting EU citizens in the digital world, I think that it should address another problem that plagues the gaming community and tarnishes consumer rights - the destruction of video games. There are many one time purchase video games that require an internet connection to a centralized server and when the server is inevitably shut down, the game you have bought is permanently disabled. On purchase you are never warned when or if this would happen. An example is "The Crew", a 2014 game, which was shut down around a year ago. The price at release was 60.00. Upon server shutdown players were robbed of their purchase. There is a European citizen's initiative that has more than 1 400 000 signatures already that aims to stop this malpractice. People supporting this initiative have documented ~350 games that have been stolen from consumers in this way. ~200 more were saved by passionate fans, aiming to preserve the games they love by painstakingly reverse engineering servers most of the time. Only 36 were preserved by the developers themselves. Without legislation, obviously the industry has no plans to preserve games people have already bought. Without said legislation, as of today, around 380 games are at risk of being shut down. Please support our cause and protect consumers. Thank you!
